domain_assessments.key_development: OFAC's dual-track Cuba tightening/Venezuela GL revision alongside Lukoil GL 131E May 30 cliff creates multiple structural enforcement boundaries that evasion networks will exploit.
domain_assessments.reasoning: The weekly findings reveal sanctions architecture under pressure from timed GL expiries and recalibrations, but the architecture-over-incident principle identifies the persistent risk of intermediary adaptation (UAE/Turkey/Malaysia for Venezuela/Cuba; EU/Asia for Lukoil) as the dominant signal. Kuwait's grey-listing exposes Gulf exchange house networks as enduring evasion nodes. These are structural pressure points, not episodic enforcement wins.

domain_assessments.key_development: EU AMLA's July 2026 technical standards deadline coincides with persistent post-CJEU BO register access fragmentation.
domain_assessments.reasoning: AMLA operationalization advances EU BO framework but the shift to 'legitimate interest' access creates structural fragmentation that undermines interconnection. PNG grey-listing confirms Pacific BO opacity as a separate architectural gap. No evidence of operational effectiveness closing the legislative-reality gap.

domain_assessments.key_development: US OFAC gatekeeper enforcement intensifies while PNG/Kuwait grey-listings expose capacity/choice deficits in Pacific/Gulf enabler jurisdictions.
domain_assessments.reasoning: US professional enabler scrutiny represents choice-driven structural improvement, but PNG (capacity deficit) and Kuwait (effectiveness gaps despite frameworks) grey-listings confirm persistent enabler-jurisdiction architecture. The US enforcement shift to gatekeeper networks is analytically significant but Tier A enablement patterns persist.

domain_assessments.key_development: Mexican cartel diversification into huachicol/crude smuggling creates new commodity-finance architecture intersecting dark-fleet infrastructure.
domain_assessments.reasoning: Cartel commodity revenue diversification represents conflict-finance architecture evolution beyond narcotics, with state-adjacent Mexican fuel infrastructure enabling scale. Venezuelan post-Maduro crude networks retain residual evasion capacity. Lukoil/Rosneft revenue persistence funds Russian conflict economy.

domain_assessments.domain_name: Crypto, Digital Assets, and Financial Innovation
domain_assessments.key_development: EU AMLR establishes EUR 1,000 CASP CDD threshold with July 2026 AMLA technical standards deadline.
domain_assessments.reasoning: AMLR/MiCA intersection creates dual-regulatory architecture constraining EU crypto illicit flows. Self-hosted wallet controls address known evasion vector. Non-EU CASP gaps persist but EU framework trajectory is structurally tightening.

jurisdiction_risk_assessments.key_finding: OFAC GL cliffs (Lukoil May 30) and gatekeeper enforcement signal structural tightening but create adaptation windows.
AMLA operational with July 2026 deadlines despite BO access fragmentation.
Post-Maduro GL revision creates evasion arbitrage for residual crude networks.
FATF grey-listing exposes extractive BO opacity and capacity deficits.
FATF grey-listing confirms exchange house/DNFBP effectiveness gaps.
Repression EO tightens but state apparatus remains primary actor.
Cartel huachicol/crude architecture enabled by state-adjacent infrastructure.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: Lukoil GL 131E May 30 cliff creates critical enforcement moment for energy sector evasion architecture.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: GL expiry forces divestiture completion or blocking exposure, but non-US intermediary reliance persists. Iranian shadow fleet pressure overlaps with Russian dark-fleet capacity. Architecture adapts faster than enforcement closes vectors.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: AMLA operational with July 2026 RTS deadline despite BO fragmentation.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: Structural progress via EBA transfer and technical standards consultations, but post-CJEU BO access model creates persistent opacity. Implementation trajectory on track but member-state convergence unproven.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: Kuwait/PNG added February 2026; list now at 22 jurisdictions.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: No removals this cycle despite Algeria/Namibia progress. PNG capacity gaps and Kuwait effectiveness failures represent distinct enabler archetypes. Next plenary June 2026.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: EU post-CJEU fragmentation persists alongside PNG opacity confirmation.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: Global trajectory remains performative: legislative progress without operational convergence. EU 'legitimate interest' model undermines interconnection; PNG extractive BO gaps exemplify capacity deficits.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: EU AMLR CASP EUR 1,000 threshold with July 2026 AMLA standards.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: MiCA/AMLR convergence constrains EU crypto flows; self-hosted wallet controls address evasion vector. Enforcement pace catching innovation via regulatory architecture.

severity_reviews.reasoning: State-adjacent Russian energy revenue persistence with multi-jurisdictional subsidiary networks and May 30 enforcement cliff.
Post-Maduro arbitrage window for dark-fleet/crude networks; Cuban repression financing seeks new channels.
Post-CJEU access model undermines legislative intent; member-state divergence creates systemic opacity.
Structural shift to professional network accountability closes historical under-enforcement vector.
Confirmed enabler-jurisdiction deficiencies with global transaction friction implications.

cross_monitor_flags.summary: Lukoil revenue persistence and Venezuelan residual crude networks sustain conflict economies.
Venezuela post-Maduro dark-fleet repositioning intersects Russian crude evasion capacity.
Cuban repression apparatus and Mexican huachicol state-adjacency confirmed.
AMLA July 2026 deadlines expose member-state supervisory convergence gaps.
Lukoil blocking and Venezuela crude re-entry create energy market signals.

analyst_briefing: FIM Week of 5 May 2026 reveals sanctions architecture under structural pressure from OFAC GL cliffs (Lukoil May 30 CRITICAL) and recalibrations (Venezuela/Cuba HIGH) that evasion networks will exploit via UAE/Turkey/Malaysia intermediaries. EU AMLA advances D2/D5 contraction despite BO fragmentation, but PNG/Kuwait grey-listings (both HIGH) confirm Pacific/Gulf enabler persistence. US gatekeeper enforcement (D3 HIGH) signals Tier A choice-driven tightening. Cartel commodity diversification (D4 HIGH) evolves conflict-finance architecture. Standing trackers: T1 Russian evasion adapting via GL arbitrage; T2 AMLA improving but fragmented; T3 Grey List worsening. Severity reviews uphold CRITICAL/HIGH ratings per architecture principle. Cross-monitor: SCEM/ERM HIGH urgency on Lukoil/Venezuela dark-fleet intersections; WDM state-capture in Cuba/Mexico. Tooling ready: 4 jurisdiction movements, 3 active schemes, 3 enforcement gaps, 3 horizon events. Architecture signals dominate episodic enforcement.
